question: How are 18 open market predictions performing mid-way through their horizons?
method: Fetch live prices via web search for all tracked tickers. Compare current vs base price. Classify as TRACKING/FLAT/AGAINST. Identify predictions needing confidence adjustment.
prediction: Most 3-month predictions still flat (too early). PRED-0017 (1-week SPY bear) likely failing (market stabilized). GLD still bullish.
falsification: If >50% of directional predictions are AGAINST at midpoint, bear thesis is wrong

key_events.GLD_crash_mar21: GLD fell -4.1% on Mar 21 — hawkish Fed, strong dollar. Gold selloff with ETF outflows. Contradicts BULL thesis (PRED-0004). However, GLD close at 426.41 = same as base, so may have already recovered.
key_events.TLT_entering_target: TLT at ~88.5, up 3.11% from 85.83 base. Entering the +3% to +8% target range (PRED-0003). Flight to safety thesis may be working.
key_events.DXY_weakening: Dollar index at 99.06, down 0.94% from 100 base. Tracking toward -3% to -7% target (PRED-0007).
key_events.VIX_stable: VIX at 26.78, roughly flat from 27. Not spiking despite market uncertainty.
key_events.PRED_0017_likely_failing: SPY flat at 648.57 with 6 days to Mar 29 deadline. Would need -2% to -5% drop in 6 trading days. Conf already reduced to 0.15.

confidence_adjustments.PRED-0004: 0.60 → 0.45 (GLD crashed -4.1% on hawkish Fed, gold thesis weakened)

results.overall_assessment: 3/13 scored TRACKING, 7/13 FLAT, 1 AGAINST, 2 IN_RANGE. Too early for 3-month predictions. Short-term PRED-0017 effectively dead. Best performer: TLT (flight to safety). Worst surprise: GLD crash — hawkish Fed is not the macro environment the BULL gold thesis assumed.

verdict: PARTIAL — too early for most 3-month predictions. TLT is the standout positive. GLD crash is a warning signal.
lesson: L-1468 (concurrent session already wrote market review lesson with geopolitical event analysis)
